title: <div>  •  Literary Duels – Players use textual evidence &amp analysis as weapons
content:
• Literary Duels – Players use textual evidence & analysis as weapons.
• Creative Combat Challenges – Memorization, improvisation, or creative problem-solving battles.

✔ Faction-Based Conflicts:
• Factions can wage war, form alliances, or sabotage opponents.
• Example: The Archivist Faction vs. The Tricksters Over Who Controls The Narrative.
• Certain items and power-ups will influence faction battles.

✔ Weekly Buffs & Debuffs from Combat:
• Winners gain XP, special privileges, or item access.
• Losers suffer debuffs or in-game consequences.

📜 Why This Works:
✔ Ensures weekly competition and engagement.
✔ Adds a tactical layer beyond just academics.
✔ Balances power dynamics between students.

📜 5. Absentee Mechanic – The Marty McFly Disappearance Effect
context: From document: RPG Character System (Fully Integrated into Weekly Modules) — chunk 452 (chunk 452/555)
